root_cause.text: The `kempf_decode_tile` function lacked sufficient bounds checking on the `src` pointer, allowing the decoder to read past the end of the input buffer and process invalid data, which subsequently caused an out-of-bounds heap write.
root_cause.evidence: The patch adds a boundary check `if (src >= src_end) return AVERROR_INVALIDDATA;` before dereferencing `*src++` inside the tile decoding loop, and tightens the initial `zsize` length check.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The `kempf_decode_tile` function lacked sufficient bounds checking on the `src` pointer, allowing the decoder to read past the end of the input buffer and process invalid data, which subsequently caused an out-of-bounds heap write.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch adds a boundary check `if (src >= src_end) return AVERROR_INVALIDDATA;` before dereferencing `*src++` inside the tile decoding loop, and tightens the initial `zsize` length check.

intent_record.intent_summary: Strengthen buffer bounds check to account for variable sub_type data requirements.
intent_record.what: The buffer size check was modified to add an extra byte to the required size depending on the value of sub_type.
intent_record.how: This ensures that the source buffer has enough data to safely proceed with decompression, preventing potential out-of-bounds reads.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add bounds check to prevent out-of-bounds buffer read.
intent_record.what: Added a bounds check to verify the source pointer against the end pointer before reading data.
intent_record.how: Prevents out-of-bounds memory access when the bit buffer runs out of data.

grouping_result.intent: Add bounds check to prevent out-of-bounds buffer read.

consistency_review.core_intent: Strengthen buffer bounds checks to prevent out-of-bounds reads.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly addresses the vulnerability by adding and strengthening bounds checks to prevent the out-of-bounds buffer reads described in the root cause.

candidates.rationale: This line dereferences and increments the 'src' pointer without first checking if it has reached the end of the input buffer, leading to an out-of-bounds read.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement dereferences the 'src' pointer without checking if it exceeds the input buffer bounds, leading to an out-of-bounds read which causes the vulnerability.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 2d66a58ccde05e764594bd7e5f0f9244634d0b2c
Author: Kostya Shishkov <kostya.shishkov@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon Feb 25 21:38:25 2013 +0100

    Go2Webinar decoder

diff --git a/libavcodec/g2meet.c b/libavcodec/g2meet.c

index 0000000000..72c062c86d

+++ b/libavcodec/g2meet.c

bic_trace_step.reason: The kempf_decode_tile function is introduced in this commit. It reads from the `src` pointer (e.g., `hdr = *src++;`, `zsize = (src[0] << 8) | src[1]; src += 2;`, `nblocks = *src++ + 1;`) without properly checking if `src` has enough remaining bytes against `src_size` or `src_end` before these operations, which leads to the out-of-bounds read and subsequent errors described in the vulnerability.
